headline: Phase 7.2: Email Verification + Brute Force Protection + E2E Specs
description:
Objective

Complete the AuthJS credential auth implementation by hardening the three remaining security gaps and adding Playwright E2E specs for all auth pages.

Background

Phase 7.1 delivered a fully working credential auth flow (sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, forgot-password, reset-password) validated end-to-end. 1059 tests passing, typecheck and lint clean.

Three Remaining Gaps

  • Gap 1 (HIGH): No email verification flow — email_verified always false; unverified users can sign in without restriction
  • Gap 2 (HIGH): No brute-force protection on sign-in — unlimited password guessing
  • Gap 3 (MEDIUM): No session invalidation after password reset — stolen sessions remain valid

Email Verification Policy (User Input)

Strict model: unverified email blocks full sign-in. User gets only verification-pending restricted state. Dev-mode bypass via AUTH_DEV_AUTO_VERIFY env flag. Never silently auto-verify in production.

Acceptance Criteria

  • email_verified set to true only after token verification
  • Unverified users blocked from full app session
  • Brute-force rate limiting active on sign-in
  • AUTH_DEV_AUTO_VERIFY bypass works only in non-production
  • All new routes covered by unit tests
  • 1059+ tests passing, coverage at or above 75%
  • Playwright E2E specs for all 5 auth pages

Task Workspace
